commit.message:
Prohibit code changes during CI investigation phase (#43)

The investigation instructions now explicitly tell the agent to only
analyze logs and report findings — not to make code changes. This
ensures the user gets to decide what to do after reviewing the
analysis, rather than having the agent silently apply fixes during
the investigation step.

commit.message:
fix(NavigationView): prevent spurious overlay flash when auto mode resizes from minimal → compact (#1316)

* Initial plan

* fix: NavigationView auto mode no longer shows brief overlay on minimal→compact resize

Root cause: _OpenNavigationPane's AnimatedContainer shared _panelKey with
_CompactNavigationPane. When transitioning from minimal to compact mode,
Flutter's GlobalKey mechanism reused the AnimatedContainer state (width=320)
causing a spurious 320→50 width animation visible as a brief overlay.

Fix:
- Add usePanelKey parameter to _OpenNavigationPane (default true)
- Pass usePanelKey: false in _buildMinimalView to prevent state reuse
- Reset _minimalPaneOpen in _resolveDisplayMode when leaving minimal mode

Also: add tests and update CHANGELOG

commit.message:
feat: use grdctl --system for RDP in bootc builds

Replace xrdp with gnome-remote-desktop system service so RDP is
available at the GDM login screen before any user session.

- generateContainerfile: embed setup-rdp.sh firstboot script and
  gnome-rdp-setup.service unit inline via printf in RUN instructions;
  enable gnome-rdp-setup.service + gnome-remote-desktop.service
- rdp.go: fall back to port 3389 without requiring prior StartRDP()
  registration so the proxy works for any running Lima instance
- rdp.html: fix error display to use IronError.backtrace() not .message
- centos-stream-10-gnome.yaml: switch to grdctl --system provisioning
- images/yellowfin/: Containerfile, setup-rdp.sh, systemd unit for
  ghcr.io/tuna-os/yellowfin:gnome50 bootc image
- .github/workflows/yellowfin.yml: CI workflow to build and push image

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: enable scrollback on replay by stripping alt screen regions

On reconnect/reload, the replay buffer contained alt screen regions
which caused xterm.js to switch to alt screen where scrollback is
disabled. The TUI cursor positioning sequences also corrupted the
normal buffer display.

Fix: Use a proper parser (stripAltScreen) that tracks alt screen
depth and handles:
- Matched enter/exit pairs (strip content between them)
- Trailing unpaired enter (strip rest of buffer)
- Orphaned exit at buffer start (from 300KB truncation)
- Multiple sequential alt screen regions
- Nested alt screen entries

Live output is unfiltered so Copilot CLI TUI works normally.
Also set explicit scrollback: 5000 (up from default 1000).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix pr_monitor_stop not closing viewer (#42)

* Fix pr_monitor_stop not closing viewer by writing STOPPED to log

The single-monitor stop path called CancelPolling() and Dispose() but
never wrote a STOPPED line to the log file. The viewer watches for this
line to know when to close, so it kept running indefinitely.

- Write STOPPED log line in single-monitor pr_monitor_stop (matching
  the stop-all path)
- Add StopPermanently() that cancels without creating a new token
- Check IsStopped flag in poll loop as belt-and-suspenders
- Clarify SKILL.md: stop monitoring should call pr_monitor_stop,
  not restart via pr_monitor_start

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make StopPermanently and Dispose thread-safe with Interlocked.Exchange

Use Interlocked.Exchange to null out _pollCts atomically in both
StopPermanently and Dispose, preventing ObjectDisposedException
when concurrent stop/shutdown paths race.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Separate log write from shutdown cleanup in NotifyShutdown

Move StopPermanently/Dispose into a separate try-catch from the
STOPPED log write so session cleanup always executes even if the
log file write fails due to IO errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Separate log write from cleanup in stop-all loop

Same pattern as NotifyShutdown: isolate the STOPPED log write so
StopPermanently/Dispose always runs even if the file append fails.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make CancelPolling thread-safe with Interlocked.Exchange

Use Interlocked.Exchange to atomically swap the CTS, dispose the
old one, and guard against ObjectDisposedException. Also skip if
IsStopped to prevent recreating a CTS after permanent shutdown.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
